1	import argparse
2	import sys
3	
4	from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
5	from .formatter import format_table, format_json
6	
7	
8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))
82	
83	
84	if __name__ == "__main__":
85	    main()

3	from typing import List

6	@dataclass
7	class Bookmark:
8	    id: str
9	    url: str
10	    title: str
11	    tags: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
12	    notes: str = ""
13	    created_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().isoformat())
14	
15	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
16	        return {
17	            "id": self.id,
18	            "url": self.url,
19	            "title": self.title,
20	            "tags": self.tags,
21	            "notes": self.notes,
22	            "created_at": self.created_at,
23	        }
24	
25	    @classmethod
26	    def from_dict(cls, data: dict) -> "Bookmark":
27	        return cls(
28	            id=data["id"],
29	            url=data["url"],
30	            title=data["title"],
31	            tags=data.get("tags", []),
32	            notes=data.get("notes", ""),
33	            created_at=data.get("created_at", datetime.now().isoformat()),
34	        )

8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))
82	
83	
84	if __name__ == "__main__":

7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "

23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)

35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)

### 3. Storage & Persistence Layer

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775383175589-qjvvsn/src/storage.py`

| Function | Signature | Purpose |
|----------|-----------|---------|

| `add_bookmark()` | `add_bookmark(url: str, title: str, tags: Optional[List[str]] = None, notes: str = "", db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Bookmark` | Create and persist new bookmark (generates UUID) |
| `delete_bookmark()` | `delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool` | Delete bookmark by ID (returns success boolean) |

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775383175589-qjvvsn/src/cli.py`

**Available Commands:**

| Command | Args | Purpose |
|---------|------|---------|
| `add` | `url` `title` `[--tags ...]` `[--notes "..."]` | Add a new bookmark |
| `list` | `[--format {table,json}]` | List all bookmarks |
| `show` | `id` | Display single bookmark |
| `delete` | `id` | Delete a bookmark |

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775383175589-qjvvsn/src/formatter.py`

```python
def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str
# Returns JSON-formatted string with proper indentation
```

### 6. Project Configuration

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775383175589-qjvvsn/pyproject.toml`

```toml
[project]
name = "bookmark-cli"
version = "1.0.0"
description = "A CLI tool for managing a local bookmark collection"
requires-python = ">=3.10"

[project.scripts]
bookmark = "src.cli:main"

[project.optional-dependencies]
test = ["pytest>=7.4.0"]
```

    - `search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]`
    - `search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]`
    - `search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]`
    - `search(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], title: str = None, tag: str = None, domain: str = None) -> List[Bookmark]` (combined search with AND logic)
  - Expects CLI integration with `search` command accepting `--title`, `--tag`, `--domain`, `--format` flags

### Key Observations:

1. **Complete CRUD layer** - All basic bookmark operations are implemented
2. **Simple JSON persistence** - File-based storage at `~/.bookmarks.json`
3. **Data validation** - Uses dataclasses with type hints
4. **CLI-ready** - Fully wired argparse with multiple commands
5. **Missing search functionality** - Tests are written but implementation doesn't exist (needs `src/search.py`)
6. **UUID generation** - Unique bookmark IDs are auto-generated using `uuid.uuid4()`
7. **ISO timestamp** - Created timestamps stored in ISO format

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""Tests for search functionality.

These tests define the expected behaviour of the search module that needs
to be implemented in src/search.py and wired into the CLI.
"""

import json
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile

import pytest

from src.models import Bookmark
from src.search import search, search_by_domain, search_by_tag, search_by_title


@pytest.fixture
def bookmarks():
    return [
        Bookmark(
            id="aaa-111",
            url="https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/index.html",
            title="Python Tutorial",
            tags=["python", "tutorial", "docs"],
            notes="Official Python tutorial",
            created_at="2026-01-01T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="bbb-222",
            url="https://github.com/runpod/runpod-python",
            title="RunPod Python SDK",
            tags=["python", "sdk", "runpod"],
            notes="RunPod serverless SDK",
            created_at="2026-01-02T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="ccc-333",
            url="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript",
            title="MDN JavaScript Guide",
            tags=["javascript", "docs", "mdn"],
            notes="Comprehensive JS reference",
            created_at="2026-01-03T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="ddd-444",
            url="https://github.com/pallets/flask",
            title="Flask Web Framework",
            tags=["python", "flask", "web"],
            notes="Lightweight WSGI web framework",
            created_at="2026-01-04T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="eee-555",
            url="https://docs.python.org/3/library/argparse.html",
            title="argparse documentation",
            tags=["python", "cli", "docs"],
            notes="Argparse module reference",
            created_at="2026-01-05T00:00:00",
        ),
    ]


# ---------- search_by_title ----------


class TestSearchByTitle:
    def test_case_insensitive_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "python")
        titles = [b.title for b in result]
        assert "Python Tutorial" in titles
        assert "RunPod Python SDK" in titles

    def test_substring_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "argparse")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "eee-555"

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "nonexistent")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search_by_tag ----------


class TestSearchByTag:
    def test_exact_tag_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "flask")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ddd-444"

    def test_shared_tag(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "docs")
        assert len(result) == 3

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "rust")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search_by_domain ----------


class TestSearchByDomain:
    def test_matches_domain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "github.com")
        assert len(result) == 2

    def test_matches_subdomain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "docs.python.org")
        assert len(result) == 2

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "stackoverflow.com")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search (combined) ----------


class TestSearchCombined:
    def test_single_criterion_title(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, title="flask")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ddd-444"

    def test_single_criterion_tag(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, tag="javascript")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ccc-333"

    def test_single_criterion_domain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, domain="developer.mozilla.org")
        assert len(result) == 1

    def test_and_logic_title_and_tag(self, bookmarks):
        # "python" in title AND tagged "sdk"
        result = search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="sdk")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "bbb-222"

    def test_and_logic_all_three(self, bookmarks):
        # title contains "python", tag "docs", domain "docs.python.org"
        result = search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="docs", domain="docs.python.org")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "aaa-111"

    def test_no_criteria_returns_all(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks)
        assert len(result) == len(bookmarks)

    def test_conflicting_criteria_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, title="flask", tag="javascript")
        assert result == []


# ---------- CLI integration ----------


class TestSearchCLI:
    def test_search_command_exists(self):
        """The CLI parser should accept the 'search' command without error."""
        from src.cli import build_parser

        parser = build_parser()
        args = parser.parse_args(["search", "--title", "python"])
        assert args.command == "search"
        assert args.title == "python"

    def test_search_format_json_flag(self):
        """The CLI should accept --format json on the search command."""
        from src.cli import build_parser

        parser = build_parser()
        args = parser.parse_args(["search", "--tag", "docs", "--format", "json"])
        assert args.command == "search"
        assert args.tag == "docs"
        assert args.format == "json"

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

# Plan: Add Search Functionality to Bookmark CLI

## Context

The bookmark CLI tool (`bookmark-cli`) supports CRUD operations (add, list, show, delete) but has no way to search or filter bookmarks. The user wants search capabilities added as a new `src/search.py` module and a `search` CLI command. A full test suite already exists at `tests/test_search.py` defining the expected behavior — our implementation must satisfy those tests.

---

## Step 1: Create `src/search.py`

**New file:** `src/search.py`

Implement 4 functions, all operating on `List[Bookmark]` and returning `List[Bookmark]`:

### `search_by_title(bookmarks, query)`
- Case-insensitive substring match: `query.lower() in b.title.lower()`
- Return list of matching bookmarks

### `search_by_tag(bookmarks, tag)`
- Exact tag match: `tag in b.tags`
- Return list of matching bookmarks

### `search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain)`
- Extract domain from `b.url` using `urllib.parse.urlparse(b.url).netloc`
- Match if `domain` appears in the netloc (handles subdomains — e.g., `"docs.python.org"` matches `docs.python.org`)
  - Test `test_matches_domain` expects `"github.com"` to match `https://github.com/...` (exact netloc)
  - Test `test_matches_subdomain` expects `"docs.python.org"` to match `https://docs.python.org/...` (full netloc match)
  - Use: `netloc == domain or netloc.endswith("." + domain)` to correctly handle both exact and subdomain cases

### `search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None)`
- Start with all bookmarks
- Apply each non-None criterion as a filter (AND logic — intersection)
- If no criteria given, return all bookmarks
- Reuse the three functions above for each filter step

**Imports needed:** `urllib.parse`, `typing.List`, `typing.Optional`, `.models.Bookmark`

---

## Step 2: Add `search` command to CLI in `src/cli.py`

### 2a: Add import
Add to existing imports at top of `src/cli.py`:
```python
from .search import search
```

### 2b: Add subparser in `build_parser()`
After the `show` subparser block (line ~39), add:
```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Search by title (substring)")
search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Search by tag (exact)")
search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Search by URL domain")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

### 2c: Add command handler in `main()`
After the `show` elif block (line ~81), add:
```python
elif args.command == "search":
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

This reuses the existing `format_table` and `format_json` from `src/formatter.py` (already imported).

---

## Files Modified

| File | Action | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `src/search.py` | **Create** | New module with 4 search functions |
| `src/cli.py` | **Edit** | Add import, subparser, and handler for `search` command |

**No other files need changes.** The models, storage, and formatter modules are reused as-is.

---

## Verification

Run from the project root:

```bash
# 1. Ensure existing tests still pass
python -m pytest tests/test_storage.py -v

# 2. Run the new search tests
python -m pytest tests/test_search.py -v

# 3. Run all tests together
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

All 26 tests should pass (11 existing in test_storage + 15 new in test_search).

33	    # --- delete ---

35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---

39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:

72	        else:

1	import json
2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional
5	
6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
44	    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive report of the codebase exploration.

## Comprehensive Codebase Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the Python CLI bookmarks tool codebase. Here are my detailed findings:

### 1. Project Structure

The project has a clean, modular structure:

```
/src/
  __init__.py          (empty)
  models.py            (Bookmark data model)
  cli.py               (CLI interface with argument parsing and commands)
  storage.py           (Persistence layer - JSON-based)
  formatter.py         (Output formatting - table and JSON)

/tests/
  __init__.py          (empty)
  test_storage.py      (Tests for storage operations)
  test_search.py       (Tests for search functionality - PLANNED)

pyproject.toml         (Project metadata and dependencies)
```

### 2. Bookmark Data Model

The `Bookmark` model is defined in `/src/models.py` as a dataclass with the following fields:

- `id`: str (unique identifier)
- `url`: str (the bookmark URL)
- `title`: str (display title)
- `tags`: List[str] (default: empty list)
- `notes`: str (default: empty string)
- `created_at`: str (ISO format timestamp, defaults to current time)

The model includes:
- `to_dict()` method to serialize to dictionary
- `from_dict()` class method to deserialize from dictionary

### 3. CLI Structure (cli.py)

The CLI is built using Python's `argparse` module with the following architecture:

**Commands implemented:**
- `add` - Add a new bookmark
  - Required args: `url`, `title`
  - Optional args: `--tags` (multiple), `--notes`
- `list` - List all bookmarks
  - Optional arg: `--format` (choices: "table", "json", default: "table")
- `delete` - Delete a bookmark by ID
  - Required arg: `id`
- `show` - Show a single bookmark
  - Required arg: `id`

**Command structure:**
- Main parser created by `build_parser()` function
- Uses subparsers for command routing
- Main entry point is `main(argv=None)` function that handles all command logic
- No search command exists yet (planned in test_search.py)

### 4. Existing Tests Structure

**test_storage.py** - Tests for persistence layer:
- `TestLoadBookmarks` - Tests loading bookmarks from JSON file
- `TestSaveBookmarks` - Tests saving bookmarks to JSON file
- `TestAddBookmark` - Tests adding and persisting new bookmarks
- `TestDeleteBookmark` - Tests deletion functionality
- `TestGetBookmark` - Tests retrieval by ID
- Uses pytest fixtures with temporary database files

**test_search.py** - Tests for PLANNED search functionality:
- Imports `search_by_title`, `search_by_tag`, `search_by_domain`, and `search` from `src.search` (module doesn't exist yet)
- Provides a comprehensive bookmark fixture with 5 test bookmarks
- Tests are organized into classes:
  - `TestSearchByTitle` - Case-insensitive substring matching in titles
  - `TestSearchByTag` - Exact tag matching
  - `TestSearchByDomain` - Domain extraction and matching from URLs
  - `TestSearchCombined` - Combined multi-criteria search with AND logic
  - `TestSearchCLI` - CLI integration tests expecting a `search` command

### 5. Contents of tests/test_search.py

The test file defines expected behavior for a `search` module that needs to be implemented:

**Test Bookmarks Fixture** (5 test bookmarks):
- Python Tutorial (docs.python.org) - tags: python, tutorial, docs
- RunPod Python SDK (github.com) - tags: python, sdk, runpod
- MDN JavaScript Guide (developer.mozilla.org) - tags: javascript, docs, mdn
- Flask Web Framework (github.com) - tags: python, flask, web
- argparse documentation (docs.python.org) - tags: python, cli, docs

**Search Functions Expected**:

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

import json
import os
import uuid
from typing import List, Optional

from .models import Bookmark

DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")


def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
        return []
    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]


def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)


def add_bookmark(
    url: str,
    title: str,
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    notes: str = "",
    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
) -> Bookmark:
    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    bookmark = Bookmark(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        url=url,
        title=title,
        tags=tags or [],
        notes=notes,
    )
    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return bookmark


def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    original_len = len(bookmarks)
    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
        return False
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return True


def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    for b in bookmarks:
        if b.id == bookmark_id:
            return b
    return None

"""Tests for search functionality.

These tests define the expected behaviour of the search module that needs
to be implemented in src/search.py and wired into the CLI.
"""

import json
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile

import pytest

from src.models import Bookmark
from src.search import search, search_by_domain, search_by_tag, search_by_title


@pytest.fixture
def bookmarks():
    return [
        Bookmark(
            id="aaa-111",
            url="https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/index.html",
            title="Python Tutorial",
            tags=["python", "tutorial", "docs"],
            notes="Official Python tutorial",
            created_at="2026-01-01T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="bbb-222",
            url="https://github.com/runpod/runpod-python",
            title="RunPod Python SDK",
            tags=["python", "sdk", "runpod"],
            notes="RunPod serverless SDK",
            created_at="2026-01-02T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="ccc-333",
            url="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript",
            title="MDN JavaScript Guide",
            tags=["javascript", "docs", "mdn"],
            notes="Comprehensive JS reference",
            created_at="2026-01-03T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="ddd-444",
            url="https://github.com/pallets/flask",
            title="Flask Web Framework",
            tags=["python", "flask", "web"],
            notes="Lightweight WSGI web framework",
            created_at="2026-01-04T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="eee-555",
            url="https://docs.python.org/3/library/argparse.html",
            title="argparse documentation",
            tags=["python", "cli", "docs"],
            notes="Argparse module reference",
            created_at="2026-01-05T00:00:00",
        ),
    ]


# ---------- search_by_title ----------


class TestSearchByTitle:
    def test_case_insensitive_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "python")
        titles = [b.title for b in result]
        assert "Python Tutorial" in titles
        assert "RunPod Python SDK" in titles

    def test_substring_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "argparse")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "eee-555"

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "nonexistent")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search_by_tag ----------


class TestSearchByTag:
    def test_exact_tag_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "flask")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ddd-444"

    def test_shared_tag(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "docs")
        assert len(result) == 3

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "rust")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search_by_domain ----------


class TestSearchByDomain:
    def test_matches_domain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "github.com")
        assert len(result) == 2

    def test_matches_subdomain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "docs.python.org")
        assert len(result) == 2

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "stackoverflow.com")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search (combined) ----------


class TestSearchCombined:
    def test_single_criterion_title(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, title="flask")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ddd-444"

    def test_single_criterion_tag(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, tag="javascript")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ccc-333"

    def test_single_criterion_domain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, domain="developer.mozilla.org")
        assert len(result) == 1

    def test_and_logic_title_and_tag(self, bookmarks):
        # "python" in title AND tagged "sdk"
        result = search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="sdk")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "bbb-222"

    def test_and_logic_all_three(self, bookmarks):
        # title contains "python", tag "docs", domain "docs.python.org"
        result = search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="docs", domain="docs.python.org")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "aaa-111"

    def test_no_criteria_returns_all(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks)
        assert len(result) == len(bookmarks)

    def test_conflicting_criteria_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, title="flask", tag="javascript")
        assert result == []


# ---------- CLI integration ----------


class TestSearchCLI:
    def test_search_command_exists(self):
        """The CLI parser should accept the 'search' command without error."""
        from src.cli import build_parser

        parser = build_parser()
        args = parser.parse_args(["search", "--title", "python"])
        assert args.command == "search"
        assert args.title == "python"

    def test_search_format_json_flag(self):
        """The CLI should accept --format json on the search command."""
        from src.cli import build_parser

        parser = build_parser()
        args = parser.parse_args(["search", "--tag", "docs", "--format", "json"])
        assert args.command == "search"
        assert args.tag == "docs"
        assert args.format == "json"

"""Search functionality for bookmarks."""

from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose title contains *query* (case-insensitive)."""
    query_lower = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if query_lower in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks that have an exact match for *tag*."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose URL hostname matches *domain*."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).hostname == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Search bookmarks by combining criteria with AND logic.

    Only criteria that are not ``None`` are applied.  If no criteria are
    given every bookmark is returned.
    """
    results = list(bookmarks)

    if title is not None:
        results = search_by_title(results, title)
    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)

    return results

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
